content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

April 5, 1991

Frederick K. Lowell  
PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO  
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 335  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No: I-91-208

Dear Mr. Lowell:

We have received your letter dated April 4, 1991, seeking written confirmation of the telephone advice Scott Hallabrin provided to you on the same day regarding the honoraria prohibitions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").¹ Because your request does not identify the name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance. (Regulation 18329.)²

Your letter accurately reflects the informal advice that Mr. Hallabrin provided to you regarding a charity golf tournament. Greens fees, meals, refreshments, transportation, and entertainment provided to elected state officers and state administrative officials in connection with the event and provided by the event sponsors are not honoraria. (Section 89502(b).) These items may be gifts and limited by Sections 89504 and 89505.

The Commission is still examining the various issues arising from the definition of honorarium and may enact regulations interpreting the honoraria provisions in the Act. Accordingly, we

---

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

are unable to assure that the advice expressed in this letter will not change as a result of further consideration of the issues.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mark T. Morodomi
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MTM:dg
Enclosure
